questions.question_text: What is the name of Idaho's seller disclosure form?
distractors: RE-16 Transfer Disclosure Statement
RE-50 Property Inspection Report
RE-10 Residential Seller Report
questions.explanation: Idaho uses the RE-25 form, formally called the Seller's Property Condition Disclosure Form, required under the Property Condition Disclosure Act (Title 55, Chapter 25).

questions.question_text: Within how many days of the offer must the seller deliver the RE-25 disclosure to the buyer?
distractors: 5 business days
7 calendar days
15 calendar days
questions.explanation: The seller must deliver the completed RE-25 form to the buyer within 10 calendar days of the offer to purchase. This gives buyers time to review property conditions before the transaction progresses.

questions.question_text: A seller knows their basement leaks during heavy rain. Are they required to disclose this on the RE-25?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, sellers must disclose all known defects
distractors: No, if the basement was recently repaired
Only if the buyer specifically asks about water
Only if the leak causes structural damage
questions.explanation: Sellers must disclose all known defects on the RE-25 form. A leaking basement is a known condition that affects the property and must be disclosed regardless of repairs or severity.

questions.question_text: Is the seller required to inspect generally inaccessible areas of the property for defects?
questions.correct_answer: No, only known defects must be disclosed
distractors: Yes, all areas must be inspected before sale
Yes, including crawl spaces and attics
Only if the buyer requests an inspection
questions.explanation: Sellers have no obligation to inspect 'generally inaccessible areas.' The RE-25 requires disclosure of known defects only — the seller discloses what they know, not what an inspection might reveal.

questions.question_text: Which areas are covered by the RE-25 property condition disclosure?
questions.correct_answer: Foundation, electrical, plumbing, heating, septic, structural
distractors: Only the roof and foundation components
Only mechanical systems less than 10 years old
Only items visible during a standard walkthrough tour
questions.explanation: The RE-25 covers many areas including foundation, electrical, heating, plumbing, septic systems, structural issues, past repairs, HOA conditions, and neighborhood conditions.

questions.question_text: After receiving the RE-25 disclosure, how many days does the buyer have to rescind?
distractors: 5 calendar days
7 calendar days
10 business days
questions.explanation: The buyer has a 3-business-day rescission right from the date they receive the property condition disclosure. This allows buyers to reconsider after learning about the property's condition.

questions.question_text: A buyer receives the RE-25 on Wednesday. By when is the last day to rescind?
questions.correct_answer: The following Monday (3 business days later)
distractors: Friday of the same week (2 days later)
Saturday of the same week (3 days later)
The following Wednesday (5 business days later)
questions.explanation: The 3-business-day rescission period starts when the buyer receives the disclosure. Counting Thursday, Friday, and Monday as business days (excluding weekend), the last day to rescind is Monday.

questions.question_text: A buyer does not receive the RE-25 until 15 days after the offer. What right does the buyer still have?
questions.correct_answer: 3 business days to rescind from date of receipt
distractors: No rescission right due to late delivery
Automatic contract cancellation
7 business days due to late delivery
questions.explanation: The buyer's 3-business-day rescission right runs from the date they actually receive the disclosure, not from the offer date. Even with late delivery, the rescission period applies from receipt.

questions.question_text: During the rescission period, the buyer discovers a significant issue in the disclosure. How should they rescind?
questions.correct_answer: Provide written notice within the 3-business-day period
distractors: Verbal notice to the seller's agent is sufficient
File a complaint with IREC
Simply stop communicating with the seller
questions.explanation: To exercise the rescission right, the buyer must provide written notice within the 3-business-day period. Verbal notice or silence does not constitute proper rescission under the Property Condition Disclosure Act.

questions.question_text: If a buyer waives the rescission period and later discovers an undisclosed defect, what remedy is available?
questions.correct_answer: Legal action against the seller for non-disclosure
distractors: No remedy once the rescission period is waived
The buyer can file an IREC complaint only
The title insurance company covers all defects
questions.explanation: Waiving the rescission period does not waive the right to sue for non-disclosure. If a seller knowingly failed to disclose a defect, the buyer may have a legal claim for damages against the seller.

questions.question_text: Which type of property sale is EXEMPT from Idaho's property condition disclosure requirement?
distractors: Sales of homes over 50 years old
Sales to first-time homebuyers
Sales involving FHA financing
questions.explanation: Court-ordered sales are exempt from the RE-25 disclosure requirement. Other exemptions include new construction (builder warranty), foreclosures, and transfers between co-owners.

questions.question_text: A new home has never been occupied. Is the builder required to provide the RE-25 disclosure?
questions.correct_answer: No, new construction is covered by builder warranty
distractors: Yes, all residential sales require the RE-25
Only if the home is over $500,000
Only if requested by the buyer
questions.explanation: New construction is exempt from the RE-25 disclosure requirement because it is covered by the builder's warranty. The builder's warranty provides its own set of protections for the buyer.

questions.question_text: A seller is asked about a death that occurred in their home. Must they disclose it?
questions.correct_answer: No, deaths in the home are not required to be disclosed
distractors: Yes, all material events must be disclosed
Only if the death was a homicide
Only if the buyer asks directly
questions.explanation: Idaho's psychologically impacted property statute (Title 55, Chapter 28) does not require sellers to disclose deaths in the home. Other non-disclosable items include HIV/AIDS status of prior occupants and sex offender proximity.

questions.question_text: Which of the following does Idaho law NOT require sellers to disclose?
distractors: Known foundation cracks
A faulty septic system
Previous water damage
questions.explanation: Idaho law does not require disclosure of sex offender proximity. However, sellers must disclose known physical defects like foundation cracks, septic problems, and water damage on the RE-25.

questions.question_text: Two co-owners transfer their property between themselves. Is the RE-25 disclosure required?
questions.correct_answer: No, transfers between co-owners are exempt
distractors: Yes, all property transfers require disclosure
Only if the property has known defects
Only if one co-owner is a licensee
questions.explanation: Transfers between co-owners are exempt from the RE-25 disclosure requirement. This exemption recognizes that co-owners already have knowledge of the property they jointly own.
